ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Filing Proof Of Transfer
Off Calendar Jan-21-2015 Continued To Feb-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Barbara Bender in Propria Person via Court Call.

Ms. Bender reported that she had been uninformed as to the transfer fees and mailed the check on January 12, 2015. The court supplied Ms. Bender with the name and telephone number of the transfer clerk. The status of filing proof of transfer was continued to February 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/ Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Benton. (Not Reported)

Petition Of Court-Appointed Guardian Ad Litem For Beneficiary To Transfer Proceedings To Sacramento County
Off Calendar Nov-25-2014 Granted Per Written Order

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jul-18-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Weaver for City National Bank (Trustee).

Mr. Weaver explained that half of the $4,600.00 monthly payment to Carl went directly to Carl and half went on payroll, that health insurance was provided to Carl and Michaela through the payroll mechanism, and that he is not prepared to answer whether Michaela qualifies for Medi Cal or Medicare. Mr. Weaver also stated that the contractor was over budget, that the choice was to have an unfinished bedroom and bathroom or to have the Trustee use discretion to pay the additional $6,000.00. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. McConico. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #4, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-12-2012 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mark Weaver for City National Bank. Barbara Bender Court appointed Guardian Ad Litem appeared by telephone for Michaela Madeiros. Roxanne Carlson (Mother of Beneficiary) and Richard Carlson (Husband of Roxanne) in Propria Persona. Vergel Tan and Vicki Koenig (Trust Officers) also present.

The Parties addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to payment of the Parents legal fees for litigation of the custody matter, work (constrcution) being done to the home by licensed Contractors, payment to the Mother for care of Michaela, Attorney's fees, cost of educating the new Trust Officer, etc. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar May-05-2010 Continued To Jun-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person.

The tentative indicated that the matter was continued. The Court wanted to see if any family members would appear. There being no appearances, the matter was continued to June 23, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Apr-28-2010 Continued To May-05-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Roxanne Carlson (Mother of Michaela Madeiros) in Propria Persona. Richard Carlson (Husband of Roxanne) in Propria Persona.

This matter was continued by the Examiner and not scheduled to be heard. Mr. and Mrs. Carlson appeared before the Court for the purpose of requesting that the Trust pay Ms. Carlson for providing weekend care for Michaela. The Parties also stated that they asked the Trustee's Attorney to include their request in his petition, that Counsel did not want to because there was not enough time, that the Trust was paying Ms. Carlson for two and a half years but that payment stopped in July of 2009 because Michaela's Father disagreed to continue paying her, that Michaela's Father was being paid out of the Trust to provide care and that they did not feel that the Father should have control over this issue. In addition, they stated that caring for Michaela was a financial hardship because Ms. Carlson was not able to work on those days due to the level of care Michaela required, and that she would continue to care for Michaela regardless of whether she got paid because she loves her Daughter and wants to be there for her. The Court directed Mr. Carlson to ask the Trustee to file the letter he to wrote to them addressing this issue. The Examiner will provide notes to Counsel so that this issue can be addressed and to see if there should be an amendment to the petition or if a separate petition should be filed. The matter was continued to May 5, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Off Calendar Jan-31-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Michael Korbholz appearing for trustee. Court asked why this is a Special Needs Trust. Counsel stated that the beneficiary is receiving benefits from the State. Accounting issue and venue issue addressed. Matter submitted.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

1st Acct & Rpt Of Trustee & Ptn For Settlement
Off Calendar Jan-10-2007 Continued To Jan-31-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
